overview: A microservices-based leave management system with a React frontend (leave-web-app) and two backend services: leave-api handles all leave request operations and user state, while manager-api provides manager-specific views and approval workflows. The web app communicates with both APIs over REST, and services are independently deployable on Kubernetes with Docker containers.
requirements: Employees must be able to submit leave requests by selecting start date, end date, and leave type
Employees must be able to view their current leave balance broken down by leave type (e.g. annual, sick)
Employees must be able to view all their submitted leave requests and their current status (pending, approved, rejected)
Employees must be able to cancel a pending leave request before a manager has made a decision
Managers must be able to view all pending leave requests from their direct reports
Managers must be able to approve or reject leave requests with an optional comment
All users must be able to authenticate and see data scoped to their role and organizational hierarchy
The system must prevent overlapping leave requests for the same employee
The system must update leave balances when requests are approved
All services must expose a /health endpoint for monitoring

components.name: leave-web-app
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Web App
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Frontend SPA for leave management
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
components.componentAgentInstructions: Build a single-page React application in TypeScript using Create React App or Vite. Serve the built static files using a lightweight Node.js express server on port 8080. Implement the following pages: (1) Employee Dashboard showing leave balance by type and a list of submitted requests with status badges, (2) Submit Leave Request form with date pickers for start/end dates and a dropdown for leave type, (3) Manager Dashboard showing pending requests from direct reports with approve/reject buttons and optional comment field. Use React Router for navigation. Call leave-api for employee operations (GET /leave/balance, POST /leave/requests, GET /leave/requests, DELETE /leave/requests/:id) and manager-api for manager operations (GET /manager/requests, POST /manager/requests/:id/approve, POST /manager/requests/:id/reject). Store user context (employeeId, role) in React context or state management library. Display error messages from API responses. Include a /health endpoint in the express server that returns 200 OK. Use environment variables for API base URLs (LEAVE_API_URL, MANAGER_API_URL). Dockerfile: multi-stage build with node:18 for build step, then node:18-alpine to run express server. EXPOSE 8080.

components.name: leave-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Backend service for employee leave operations
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
  /leave/balance:
    get:
      summary: Get leave balance for current user
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave balance by type
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  balances:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: object
                      properties:
                        leaveType:
                          type: string
                        available:
                          type: number
                        total:
                          type: number
        '400':
          description: Missing employeeId
  /leave/requests:
    get:
      summary: Get all leave requests for current user
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of leave requests
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  requests:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: object
                      properties:
                        id:
                          type: string
                        startDate:
                          type: string
                          format: date
                        endDate:
                          type: string
                          format: date
                        leaveType:
                          type: string
                        status:
                          type: string
                          enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
                        managerNote:
                          type: string
        '400':
          description: Missing employeeId
    post:
      summary: Submit a new leave request
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - employeeId
                - startDate
                - endDate
                - leaveType
              properties:
                employeeId:
                  type: string
                startDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                endDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                leaveType:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Request created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  requestId:
                    type: string
                  status:
                    type: string
        '400':
          description: Invalid input or overlapping request
        '409':
          description: Overlapping leave request exists
  /leave/requests/{id}:
    delete:
      summary: Cancel a pending leave request
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: employeeId
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '204':
          description: Request cancelled
        '400':
          description: Request is not pending or does not belong to employee
        '404':
          description: Request not found
components.componentAgentInstructions: Create a Go HTTP service using net/http on port 9090. Use an in-memory map to store leave requests (keyed by request ID, value is struct with id, employeeId, startDate, endDate, leaveType, status, managerNote). Use another map for leave balances keyed by employeeId with nested map of leaveType to available/total days. Seed initial data: employee 'emp1' with 15 annual days, 10 sick days; employee 'emp2' with 20 annual, 5 sick. GET /leave/balance?employeeId=X returns JSON with balances array. GET /leave/requests?employeeId=X returns all requests for that employee. POST /leave/requests validates JSON body, checks for overlapping dates (compare with existing requests for same employee), generates UUID for request ID, sets status to 'pending', stores request, returns 201 with requestId. Return 409 if overlap detected. DELETE /leave/requests/:id?employeeId=X checks the request belongs to employee and status is pending, then removes it, returns 204. Return 400 if not pending or wrong employee. GET /health returns 200 OK with body {"status":"ok"}. Use gorilla/mux or standard ServeMux for routing. Dockerfile: FROM golang:1.21 AS builder, WORKDIR /app, COPY go.mod go.sum ./, RUN go mod download, COPY . ., RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o leave-api, then FROM alpine:latest, COPY --from=builder /app/leave-api ., EXPOSE 9090, CMD ["./leave-api"]. Listen on 0.0.0.0:9090.

components.name: manager-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Manager API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Backend service for manager leave approval workflows
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
  /manager/requests:
    get:
      summary: Get pending leave requests for direct reports
      parameters:
        - name: managerId
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of pending requests from direct reports
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  requests:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: object
                      properties:
                        id:
                          type: string
                        employeeId:
                          type: string
                        employeeName:
                          type: string
                        startDate:
                          type: string
                          format: date
                        endDate:
                          type: string
                          format: date
                        leaveType:
                          type: string
                        status:
                          type: string
        '400':
          description: Missing managerId
  /manager/requests/{id}/approve:
    post:
      summary: Approve a leave request
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - managerId
              properties:
                managerId:
                  type: string
                note:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Request approved
        '400':
          description: Invalid request or manager not authorized
        '404':
          description: Request not found
  /manager/requests/{id}/reject:
    post:
      summary: Reject a leave request
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - managerId
              properties:
                managerId:
                  type: string
                note:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Request rejected
        '400':
          description: Invalid request or manager not authorized
        '404':
          description: Request not found
components.componentAgentInstructions: Create a Go HTTP service using net/http on port 9090. Maintain an in-memory organizational hierarchy map: managerId -> list of employeeIds (e.g. 'mgr1' manages ['emp1', 'emp2']). Share or replicate leave request state from leave-api (for simplicity, use an in-memory map here too, or call leave-api via HTTP if you want true separation—prefer in-memory for this implementation). GET /manager/requests?managerId=X filters all pending leave requests where employeeId is in the manager's direct reports list, returns JSON with requests array including employeeName (mock names like 'Employee 1'). POST /manager/requests/:id/approve with JSON body {managerId, note?} checks that the request's employeeId is managed by managerId, updates request status to 'approved', sets managerNote if provided, deducts leave days from employee's balance (call internal function or update in-memory balance map), returns 200. POST /manager/requests/:id/reject similarly sets status to 'rejected', sets managerNote, does not deduct balance, returns 200. Return 400 if manager is not authorized for that employee. Return 404 if request ID not found. GET /health returns 200 OK {"status":"ok"}. Use gorilla/mux or ServeMux. Dockerfile: same multi-stage pattern as leave-api (golang:1.21 builder, alpine runtime, EXPOSE 9090, CMD ["./manager-api"]). Listen on 0.0.0.0:9090. Seed hierarchy: mgr1 -> [emp1, emp2]. If you choose to call leave-api over HTTP instead of replicating state, use http.Get/Post to LEAVE_API_URL environment variable, but in-memory replication is simpler for this exercise.
